deep_dive.title: A Survey on Federated Causal Discovery and Inference
deep_dive.abstract: Causal reasoning, which encompasses the discovery of causal structures and the inference of causal effects, is fundamental to data-driven decision making. In practice, data for reliable causal analysis are often distributed across institutions and cannot be centralized due to privacy regulations or communication constraints. Federated learning (FL) addresses this by enabling collaborative analysis without raw data sharing, giving rise to the rapidly growing field of federated causal discovery (FCD) and inference (FCI). However, the interdisciplinary nature of this field and the absence of a comprehensive survey present barriers to entry for researchers. This paper bridges that gap by providing a systematic review through multi-dimensional taxonomies. Grounded in the three core design decisions underlying any FCD solution, namely how structures are learned, how data are partitioned, and what structural knowledge each party obtains, we organize FCD along three axes: methodological paradigm, federation topology, and structural scope. We further examine key practical dimensions, including temporal dynamics, data heterogeneity, missing data, and non-identical variable sets. For FCI, we categorize methods by target estimand (average versus individualized/conditional treatment effects) and by estimation strategy, from classical weighting methods to modern deep generative architectures. Unlike prior works that treat FCD and FCI separately, we formalize their connection as complementary stages of a unified federated causal reasoning pipeline, where FCD supplies the structural knowledge required for valid effect estimation in FCI. Finally, we highlight their shared concerns regarding privacy, communication efficiency, theoretical guarantees, and application domains, and conclude by identifying open challenges for future research.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: comprehensive survey on federated causal discovery and inference

deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对联邦学习中因果发现与推断的交叉领域缺乏系统性综述的问题，提出多维分类法。综述了联邦设置下的因果结构学习和因果效应推断方法，涵盖算法、隐私和通信约束。为研究者提供了全面的参考框架。

deep_dive.title: Uncovering dynamic human brain phase coherence networks
deep_dive.abstract: Complex cognitive functions rely on coordinated communication between distributed brain regions, yet capturing these interactions as they evolve over time remains challenging. Traditional analyses of functional brain connectivity largely rely on correlations in signal amplitude, which are sensitive to noise and artifacts such as head motion. Here, we introduce a mixture modeling approach that focuses on the phase of brain signals, allowing dynamic patterns of large-scale synchronization in brain phase coherence networks to be studied directly and in their entirety. We lay the mathematical and conceptual groundwork for phase modeling and introduce the complex angular central Gaussian mixture model, providing a principled way to analyze phase-based interactions across the brain. Applied to fMRI data, the model identifies recurring states of brain-wide synchronized activity that reliably distinguish cognitive tasks and generalize across previously unseen individuals, without requiring any task labels during training. These results show that modeling signal phase offers a clean and informative view of brain synchronization dynamics, opening new avenues for studying large-scale neural coordination.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: mixture model for phase coherence dynamics in brain signals
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于脑信号相位一致动态的混合模型
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用于脑信号相位一致动态的混合模型
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用于脑信号相位一致动态的混合模型
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces a mixture modeling approach for dynamic brain phase coherence networks, overcoming amplitude correlation limitations.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对传统脑功能连接分析依赖振幅相关性的局限性，本文提出一种基于相位信号的混合建模方法，利用复杂角中心高斯混合模型研究大规模同步的动态模式。该方法直接从相位数据捕捉脑区间交互，避免了运动伪影干扰，为理解认知功能提供了新途径。

deep_dive.title: A Causal Foundation Model for Structure and Outcome Prediction
deep_dive.abstract: We introduce TabPFN-CFM, a causal foundation model that can handle multiple causal problems. TabPFN-CFM predicts both causal structure and outcomes from observational data, supports queries on all three levels of Pearl's Causal Hierarchy and uses known graph structure when available to improve predictions. TabPFN-CFM is trained on synthetic datasets, and generalises to real datasets, demonstrating improved performance over both structural and outcome prediction baselines.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: causal foundation model for structure and outcome prediction
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于结构和结果预测的因果基础模型
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用于结构和结果预测的因果基础模型
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用于结构和结果预测的因果基础模型
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces TabPFN-CFM, a causal foundation model trained on synthetic data that predicts both causal structure and outcomes from observational data, generalizing to real datasets.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有因果推断方法通常针对特定任务，缺乏通用性。本文提出TabPFN-CFM，一个基于transformer的因果基础模型，在合成数据上训练，能够从观测数据中同时预测因果结构和因果结果，支持Pearl因果层次的所有查询。在真实数据集上，该模型在结构和结果预测上均超越基准，展示了基础模型在因果学习中的巨大潜力。

quick_skim.title: Speaking Numbers to LLMs: Multi-Wavelet Number Embeddings for Time Series Forecasting
quick_skim.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are attractive for context-aware time series forecasting because they can integrate heterogeneous textual signals, yet their discrete, language-oriented tokenization and embedding interfaces are misaligned with continuous numerical values, often harming numerical ordering and forecasting reliability. We propose TempoWave, a plug-and-play temporal wavelet digit interface that maps each scalar observation into digit-wise embeddings constructed from multi-wavelet, multi-scale coefficients. By directly overriding standard token representations, TempoWave seamlessly exposes both fine-grained local fluctuations and macro global structures in a transformer-compatible form, ensuring that precise numerical formatting, distinct digit identity, and robustness to common normalization operations are maintained throughout the LLM pipeline. Experiments across five context-enriched forecasting benchmarks demonstrate that TempoWave consistently improves LLM-based forecasters over standard numeric tokenization and alternative embedding interfaces, achieving a new state-of-the-art. These results highlight the numeric interface as a key bottleneck and suggest that principled multi-resolution embeddings can better couple LLMs' contextual reasoning with precise forecasting. Our code is available at https://github.com/DC-research/TempoWAVE and our model can be accessed at https://huggingface.co/Melady/TempoWAVE.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: uses LLMs with multi-wavelet embedding for time series forecasting, relevant to frozen encoder LLM time series
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用大语言模型和多小波嵌入进行时间序列预测，与冻结编码器大语言模型时间序列相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用大语言模型和多小波嵌入进行时间序列预测，与冻结编码器大语言模型时间序列相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用大语言模型和多小波嵌入进行时间序列预测，与冻结编码器大语言模型时间序列相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes TempoWave, a multi-wavelet digit embedding interface that adapts LLMs for time series forecasting by converting scalars to wavelet-based token embeddings.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 大语言模型在时间序列预测中因离散分词与连续数值不匹配而性能受限。本文提出TempoWave，将每个标量观测映射为多小波数字嵌入，直接替换标准token表示，使LLM能同时感知局部波动和全局结构。该方法即插即用，在多个预测任务上显著提升LLM的数值理解与预测可靠性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 大语言模型在时间序列预测中因离散分词与连续数值不匹配而性能受限。本文提出TempoWave，将每个标量观测映射为多小波数字嵌入，直接替换标准token表示，使LLM能同时感知局部波动和全局结构。该方法即插即用，在多个预测任务上显著提升LLM的数值理解与预测可靠性。

quick_skim.title: Attractive and Repulsive Pattern Control in Sequence Generation
quick_skim.abstract: Variable-order Markov models preserve local symbolic syntax by adapting context length, but long continuations can enter recurring high-order "tunnels": repeated suffixes, locally periodic passages, or copied fragments longer than the formal Markov order. This paper introduces signed pattern control for variable-order Markov generation with BP-Regular sampling. A weighted recurrence automaton computes an activation R for a chosen family of target patterns, and belief propagation samples exactly from P_beta(x) proportional to P_0(x) exp(beta R(x)). Negative coupling makes the target patterns costly during sampling; positive coupling rewards the same patterns and turns them into controlled attractors.   The target family may be mined online from overactive generated material, supplied by a score or style vocabulary, or designed as an experimental probe. The main experiments use the online homeostatic case, choosing patterns that become overactive in the sampling history. On six duration-bearing monophonic sources, including Bach and Telemann material, the negative branch reduces generated 8-gram self-reuse, increases the effective number of generated 8-grams, and increases coverage of training-supported 4-gram contexts while preserving substantial lower-order support. A pitch-sequence replication on five Weimar Jazz Database solos gives the same anti-reuse signature outside Baroque material. The same signed mechanism also provides a positive branch for probing attractor basins, phase transitions, and hysteresis in the underlying variable-order model.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 带有模式控制的序列生成
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 带有模式控制的序列生成
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 带有模式控制的序列生成
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces signed pattern control for variable-order Markov sequence generation using belief propagation for exact sampling.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对变阶马尔可夫模型在长序列生成中容易出现重复后缀、局部周期等模式隧道问题，本文提出带符号模式控制方法。通过加权递归自动机计算目标模式的激活值，并利用信念传播从修正分布中精确采样，实现对生成序列中目标模式的吸引或排斥。该方法为时间序列生成提供了灵活的模式控制机制，能够有效避免不良模式或引导生成特定模式。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对变阶马尔可夫模型在长序列生成中容易出现重复后缀、局部周期等模式隧道问题，本文提出带符号模式控制方法。通过加权递归自动机计算目标模式的激活值，并利用信念传播从修正分布中精确采样，实现对生成序列中目标模式的吸引或排斥。该方法为时间序列生成提供了灵活的模式控制机制，能够有效避免不良模式或引导生成特定模式。

quick_skim.title: Forecasting With LLMs: Improved Generalization Through Feature Steering
quick_skim.abstract: Successful forecasting involves identifying patterns between historical and future states of the world which generalize to future observations. We apply LLMs to a variety of forecasting tasks and inspect their internal states using sparse autoencoders to understand whether they appear to rely on time-specific pieces of knowledge versus generalizable patterns. Our analyses identify features associated with both time-aware reasoning and look-ahead-biased reasoning. We then apply the LLMs to an entirely different domain and intervene on these features. We find that amplifying time-awareness features substantially reduces look-ahead bias on forecasting prompts while preserving general reasoning performance. In contrast, steering the candidate look-ahead-bias features does not produce an effect. These results suggest that interpretable temporal features can be used to causally shift LLMs toward more historically grounded reasoning.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: uses LLM for forecasting with feature steering, relates to LLM for time series and frozen encoder concepts
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用大语言模型进行预测并通过特征干预提升泛化，与大语言模型时间序列及冻结编码器概念相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用大语言模型进行预测并通过特征干预提升泛化，与大语言模型时间序列及冻结编码器概念相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用大语言模型进行预测并通过特征干预提升泛化，与大语言模型时间序列及冻结编码器概念相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Applies LLMs to forecasting tasks, identifies time-aware features via sparse autoencoders, and steers them to reduce look-ahead bias while preserving reasoning.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 大语言模型在预测任务中可能依赖未来信息造成偏差。本文使用稀疏自编码器解析LLM内部特征，发现时间感知和前瞻偏差两类特征。通过干预放大时间感知特征，在跨域预测中显著降低前瞻偏差且保持推理能力。这展示了利用冻结LLM进行时间序列预测的改进方向，为时间序列特征挖掘提供了新思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 大语言模型在预测任务中可能依赖未来信息造成偏差。本文使用稀疏自编码器解析LLM内部特征，发现时间感知和前瞻偏差两类特征。通过干预放大时间感知特征，在跨域预测中显著降低前瞻偏差且保持推理能力。这展示了利用冻结LLM进行时间序列预测的改进方向，为时间序列特征挖掘提供了新思路。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 检索 ArXiv 今日发布的论文中，关于‘利用大语言模型并冻结其编码器（Frozen Encoder）’以及‘时间序列性质/特征挖掘（Time Series Property Mining）’的相关文章。, especially work that connects or combines: frozen encoder; large language model with frozen encoder; time series property mining; time series feature extraction; frozen encoder for time series; large language model frozen encoder; frozen encoder large language model time series; time series property mining using frozen pretrained models; frozen encoder for time series feature extraction.
